taskDescription: Fix systemic ecosystem state.json drift across BCOS-enabled repos by replacing additive-merge with disk-scan regeneration, wiring into install/daily/monthly checkpoints, and adding ecosystem-state-refresh to the auto-fix whitelist.

relevantLessons: L-INIT-20260404-009: Discovery scripts are the source of truth for what exists. State files record what discovery found, not the definition itself.
L-INIT-20260404-002: Context rot is the natural state. Without active maintenance, all business context degrades over time.

phases: Phase 1: Design refresh_ecosystem_state.py specification + classification rule
Phase 3: Wire into update.py + job-index-health.md (install + daily)
Phase 4: Wire into auto-fix whitelist + schedule-config.template + job-architecture-review.md (monthly defense)
Phase 5: FIXED END — Integration audit + ecosystem state refresh + learnings capture

tasks.content: Define classification rule: skill = directory in .claude/skills/ containing SKILL.md; agent = directory in .claude/agents/ containing AGENT.md; utility = directory in either tree without those markers. Document in script docstring.

Define output schema: inventory.skills.{total,list,overlayCapable,discoveryCapable}, inventory.agents.{total,byCategory}, inventory.utilities.{total,skills,agents}. Preserve user-authored fields (overlayCapable, discoveryCapable, health, maintenanceBacklog) — don't overwrite, only top up.
Define agent category inference: read AGENT.md frontmatter `category` field. Default to 'Uncategorized' if missing. Preserve existing byCategory placement if user manually moved an agent.
Create .claude/scripts/refresh_ecosystem_state.py — disk scan via Path.glob('.claude/skills/*/SKILL.md') and equivalent for agents. Output to state.json.

Implement preservation logic: read existing state.json first, preserve overlayCapable, discoveryCapable arrays (filtered to only contain entries that still exist on disk), preserve health + maintenanceBacklog verbatim.
Implement utilities classification: scan .claude/skills/ and .claude/agents/ for directories WITHOUT marker file; record in inventory.utilities section.
Add CLI flags: --dry-run (print diff, don't write), --json (output result as JSON), --quiet (no output unless changes). Default = apply + print summary line.
Test in dev repo: run script, confirm output matches current curated state.json (13 skills, 1 agent) plus a new utilities section with skill-discovery + agent-discovery.
tasks.activeForm: Testing refresh script in dev repo

tasks.content: Modify update.py: replace merge_ecosystem_state() call (line 730) with subprocess invocation of refresh_ecosystem_state.py. Print '  state.json: refreshed from disk (N skills, M agents, K utilities)' in summary.
Decide fate of merge_ecosystem_state() function: leave it as dead code with deprecation comment OR remove it. Recommend removal — additive merge was the original bug, no other caller.

Modify job-index-health.md: add Step 6 'Refresh ecosystem state' after document index rebuild. Steps: invoke refresh_ecosystem_state.py, capture summary, surface count of changed entries in `notes`. If script errors, log as non-fatal warning (don't fail the whole job).
Verify: run schedule-dispatcher's index-health job manually in dev repo. Confirm state.json gets updated lastUpdated to today, no other unintended changes.

Modify auto-fix-whitelist.md: add ecosystem-state-refresh fix ID with detection (state.json doesn't match disk-scan output) and fix (run refresh_ecosystem_state.py). Justify with the 5 safety criteria (deterministic, no business-content change, idempotent, easy to audit, reversible by git).
Modify schedule-config.template.json: add 'ecosystem-state-refresh' to default auto_fix.whitelist array. New deployments get the fix enabled by default.

Modify job-architecture-review.md: (a) fix --staged -> --ci typo at line 47; (b) replace ad-hoc 'check that state.json matches what actually exists' bullet with 'invoke ecosystem-state-refresh fix if drift detected'; (c) remove the explicit 'Does not modify state.json' line at 142-143; (d) update verdict logic so ecosystem drift no longer auto-triggers red (the auto-fix resolves it).

Document the design decision: add a section in document-standards.md or a new architecture note explaining 'state.json is a derived artifact, not authored truth' — invoking lesson L-INIT-20260404-009 explicitly.
FIXED END: Run python .claude/scripts/analyze_integration.py --ci against the change. Resolve any flagged coverage gaps before commit.
FIXED END: Run python .claude/scripts/refresh_ecosystem_state.py in dev repo. Verify state.json now includes utilities section. Commit the regenerated state.json as part of the change.

FIXED END: Capture lessons. Add to lessons.json: 'L-ECOSYSTEM-20260501: A state file that exists to mirror disk should be regenerated from disk, never authored. When authored, drift is inevitable. — applies to: any cache, registry, or inventory file derived from filesystem state.'
FIXED END: Verify CI checks pass. Run validate_frontmatter.py, validate_references.py, and analyze_integration.py --ci. All must pass before commit.
